title: The Real Housewives Of Fat Tony

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening scene, Homer experiences some off-screen mishap
jokes.punchline: Homer's iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfectly character-integrated and economical but not original within the show's context

jokes.text: but now my license is expired. Hey Dad, there's an arcade across the street.
jokes.setup: Homer waited in line to renew his license
jokes.punchline: The license expired while waiting in the DMV line to renew it
jokes.explanation: Classic bureaucratic irony - well-structured but fairly predictable DMV humor

jokes.text: All that's left is banana Runts. That's the worst Runt.
jokes.setup: Someone brought a gift bag to share
jokes.punchline: Only the universally disliked banana candy remains
jokes.explanation: Relatable candy hierarchy observation with good economy

jokes.text: What am I bid for next in line? Five bucks. Ten bones. Half a cheesesteak. I'll perform at your birthday party. Sober. Quarter of a cheesesteak. A nest with a robin's egg. The wrapper of a cheesesteak.
jokes.setup: Auction for a place in line
jokes.punchline: Escalating desperation from reasonable money to increasingly absurd offers
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from practical to absurd, with the 'sober' parenthetical and cheesesteak progression being particularly clever

jokes.text: FAT TONY: I bid one lollipop. Sold, to the lowest bidder.
jokes.setup: Elaborate auction with increasingly desperate bids
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony wins with the smallest, most childish offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Fat Tony's understated power move subverts the escalating desperation

jokes.text: Hello, 'Selma.' Leaving Mockingbird Lane, Mr. Munster?
jokes.setup: Fat Tony addresses Selma formally at the DMV
jokes.punchline: Selma makes a Munsters reference about Fat Tony's appearance/address
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference that fits Selma's character but relies on external reference

jokes.text: And if I retrieve that form, will I be able to maintain my advantageous position in line? Try it and see. Nope.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony asks if getting the correct form will preserve his line position
jokes.punchline: Selma's matter-of-fact 'Nope' after he loses his place
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and deadpan delivery - the bureaucratic trap executed with surgical precision

jokes.text: Hey, you licked the back. That was my side. Aw, brother germs! Aw, sister spit!
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa are sharing something that requires licking
jokes.punchline: Sibling disgust at sharing germs despite being related
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling dynamic - well-executed character comedy but predictable

jokes.text: Bart, this is a truffle. You're a truffle.
jokes.setup: Lisa tries to educate Bart about truffles
jokes.punchline: Bart's nonsensical comeback
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - childish and illogical but completely in character

jokes.text: Only you can make tree poop boring.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains truffles grow in tree roots and are prized gourmet food
jokes.punchline: Bart reduces the explanation to 'tree poop' and calls Lisa boring
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bart line - perfectly captures his ability to deflate Lisa's intellectualism with crude but effective observations

jokes.text: Come on, numb nose. Sniff me a truffle.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony has acquired Bart as a truffle-hunting 'pig'
jokes.punchline: Treating Bart like an actual pig with the dismissive 'numb nose' nickname
jokes.explanation: Good character voice for Fat Tony - the casual cruelty fits his personality

jokes.text: I don't know why I flew you in from Italy. First class, no less. Plus you forget to get the miles.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony is disappointed in his truffle pig
jokes.punchline: Complaining about airline miles on a pig's ticket
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail about treating a pig like a business traveler - the airline miles complaint is particularly funny

jokes.text: You know-a the rule: no truffles for truffle pigs.
jokes.setup: Bart has been finding truffles for the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Luigi won't let the truffle-finder eat the truffles he finds
jokes.explanation: Good irony - the worker who finds the valuable ingredient can't enjoy it, delivered in Luigi's characteristic accent

jokes.text: Not one that goes a-tikit-a-tikit-a-tikit-a.
jokes.setup: Luigi promises Bart the best table when he dines
jokes.punchline: Describes a wobbly table with onomatopoeia in Italian accent
jokes.explanation: Charming character moment - Luigi's accent makes the wobbly table sound musical

jokes.text: Fine. I choose my love handles. What? Then my arm wattles, my cankles, and finish off with my excess back fat.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony threatens to cut off body parts
jokes.punchline: Selma lists body parts she wants removed via liposuction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - turns a death threat into a cosmetic surgery consultation, perfectly fitting Selma's vanity

jokes.text: You asked what I wanted cut off, and I want lipo. Lots of lipo. Or are you not a man of your word?
jokes.setup: Selma continues treating the threat as a spa consultation
jokes.punchline: Challenges Fat Tony's honor to get free plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Selma's fearlessness and vanity turn a mob threat into negotiation - excellent character consistency

jokes.text: I don't know whether to knock you on your kisser or kiss you on your knockers. I don't know whether to peck you on the kisser, or kiss you on the...
jokes.setup: Fat Tony is charmed by Selma's boldness
jokes.punchline: Gets tongue-tied trying to make a clever rhyming threat/compliment
jokes.explanation: Shows Fat Tony flustered by Selma - the stumbling over his own wordplay is endearing

jokes.text: Call our doctor friend who owes us a favor. Actually, we owe him a favor. Do him two favors, then remind him that he owes us a favor.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony wants to arrange plastic surgery
jokes.punchline: Mob accounting of favors becomes increasingly convoluted
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how mob favor-trading can become absurdly complex

jokes.text: so can I take your cousin, the 'anesthesiologist' off the payroll?
jokes.setup: Surgery was successful
jokes.punchline: Air quotes around 'anesthesiologist' implies unqualified mob relative
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mob nepotism in medical practice - the air quotes make the joke

jokes.text: Should we take care of him, or 'take care of him'? Take care of him. I don't know what that meant. Was it the first one or the second one? I can't ask him, otherwise he's gonna 'take care of me.' Now to take care of you. Take care of me or 'take care of me'?
jokes.setup: Mob euphemisms create confusion
jokes.punchline: Extended confusion over whether 'take care of' means help or kill
jokes.explanation: Classic mob language confusion gag - runs a bit long but builds nicely

jokes.text: The world's a different place when you've got a waist.
jokes.setup: Selma has had liposuction
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation about weight loss with wordplay
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay and character moment - Selma's new confidence expressed cleverly

jokes.text: Well, well, well, if it isn't 'before' and 'after.' Blob and Blob Lite. Tweedle Yuck and Tweedle Blecch.
jokes.setup: Homer encounters Patty and the transformed Selma
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly creative insults comparing the sisters
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer insult escalation - each comparison gets more creative and cruel

jokes.text: All will be forgiven. If you sketch a portrait of Patty showing her inner beauty.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony offers forgiveness for Homer's insults
jokes.punchline: Impossible task of finding inner beauty in Patty
jokes.explanation: Cruel but clever punishment - asking Homer to find something that doesn't exist

jokes.text: I'm trying. I swear I'm trying. Damn it.
jokes.setup: Homer attempts to draw Patty's inner beauty
jokes.punchline: Homer's visible frustration at the impossible task
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the impossible task setup - Homer's struggle is the visual gag

jokes.text: The way the sun goes down like a crooked boxer.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony waxing poetic about the sunset
jokes.punchline: Romantic imagery filtered through mob sensibility
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing - Fat Tony's attempt at poetry reveals his worldview

jokes.text: Oh Tony, if there were an Italian word for 'yes,' I'd be saying it right now.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony proposes to Selma
jokes.punchline: Selma's ridiculous claim that Italian has no word for 'yes'
jokes.explanation: Absurd cultural ignorance played completely straight - very quotable

jokes.text: Brown gold. Tuscan tea.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing the truffle fortune
jokes.punchline: Pretentious nicknames for what are essentially mushrooms
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Lisa's intellectual pretension about fungi

jokes.text: That's a lot of zeros.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions 0.001% interest rate
jokes.punchline: Homer focuses on the wrong aspect - quantity vs. value of zeros
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - missing the point of low interest in favor of counting zeros

jokes.text: That tick sure done swelled you up, Normalhead Joe.
jokes.setup: Someone's head is swollen
jokes.punchline: Bart's creative explanation involving a tick and creative nickname
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart creativity - 'Normalhead Joe' is a particularly inventive insult

jokes.text: He did give her the kiss of death, right? No! Oh, I decorated her car for nothing.
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony concludes
jokes.punchline: Louie expected assassination, not actual marriage
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mob expectations vs. reality - the car decoration detail makes it funnier

jokes.text: I'm her sister. And you? I bought her ping-pong table on Craig's List.
jokes.setup: Wedding guests compare their connection to the bride
jokes.punchline: Absurd contrast between family and casual online purchase
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from intimate to completely random connection

jokes.text: We haven't even gotten our salads yet.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about dessert
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical concern about meal progression
jokes.explanation: Homer's food-focused priorities in social situations

jokes.text: Lousy St. Louis Cardinals. Can't win the 1985 World Series on Classic Sports.
jokes.setup: Homer is upset about sports results
jokes.punchline: Getting angry about decades-old game replays
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer absurdity - treating reruns like live games with stakes

jokes.text: Fat Tony! Are you going to cut my head off and leave it in a manger scene at Christmas, and then an old Italian nun finds it? And she's all, 'Oh, no, no, no! Oh, no! He's too beautiful to die!'
jokes.setup: Homer fears mob retaliation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate, specific scenario including nun commentary on his attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and escalating fear scenario - the nun's reaction to Homer's beauty is the perfect absurd detail

jokes.text: Um, I take a special size.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony says they have towels
jokes.punchline: Homer's euphemistic way of saying he's too fat for normal towels
jokes.explanation: Homer's polite way of acknowledging his size - characteristic self-awareness

jokes.text: Ooh. Commentary by Philip Glass.
jokes.setup: Lisa is excited about the art film
jokes.punchline: Gets excited about the composer's commentary track
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Lisa's pretentious film appreciation

jokes.text: There's nothing buried here but hopes and dreams.
jokes.setup: They're searching their own property for truffles
jokes.punchline: Homer's unexpectedly poetic and melancholy observation
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Homer profundity - unexpectedly beautiful and sad

jokes.text: You know what's a bad pizza topping? Broccoli!
jokes.setup: Lisa explains why she's been eating the truffles - vegetarian food is boring
jokes.punchline: Lisa's anti-vegetarian rant while high on mushrooms
jokes.explanation: Lisa temporarily abandoning her principles under influence - good character subversion

jokes.text: Oh, so that's why they don't give pigs truffles.
jokes.setup: Earlier establishment that truffle pigs don't get to eat truffles
jokes.punchline: Bart realizes the rule exists because truffles are apparently addictive/intoxicating
jokes.explanation: Good callback that explains earlier setup - Bart connects the dots

jokes.text: The only husband of Selma's I liked was Disco Stu. He was so upbeat-- till he found out she didn't care for disco.
jokes.setup: Marge discussing Selma's previous marriages
jokes.punchline: Disco Stu's relationship ended over musical preferences
jokes.explanation: Good character callback - perfectly logical reason for their breakup

jokes.text: Can you read by it? No one's ever tried.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about reading by tanning lights
jokes.punchline: Admission that literacy isn't a priority in this household
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery about household priorities, good cultural commentary

jokes.text: But not to worry-- every light in the house is a tanning light. Can you read by it? No one's ever tried.
jokes.setup: House modification to help pale guests
jokes.punchline: Practical question reveals the absurdity - lights optimized for tanning, not utility
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline revealing the impracticality of vanity-focused design

jokes.text: You married a pig. Well, you married someone whose first name is Fat.
jokes.setup: Sisters arguing about their marriage choices
jokes.punchline: Escalating insults about their respective spouses
jokes.explanation: Good sister dynamic - both insults land effectively

jokes.text: Boo-freaking-ya! Losers chug! Well, if you insist.
jokes.setup: Mob guys celebrating and making others drink as punishment
jokes.punchline: Homer treats forced drinking as hospitality
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to social dynamics - treats hazing as generosity

jokes.text: You are such a gentle kisser. You were kind of all over the place.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment between newlyweds
jokes.punchline: Awkward post-kiss critique
jokes.explanation: Awkward romantic feedback - mildly funny relationship dynamic

jokes.text: Wouldn't they be girls? Mine are boys.
jokes.setup: Discussion about something needing to breathe
jokes.punchline: Mob boss clarifying the gender of whatever needs breathing room
jokes.explanation: Context unclear but seems to be innuendo about body parts - mild humor

jokes.text: You know-- it had that painting of that lady and the monster on the ceiling. That was a mirror.
jokes.setup: Homer describing their room by a painting
jokes.punchline: It wasn't a painting - Homer saw his and Marge's reflection
jokes.explanation: Great self-deprecating humor - Homer inadvertently insults both himself and Marge

jokes.text: Why go out for a hamburger when you got rancid steak at home?
jokes.setup: Questioning why Fat Tony would cheat
jokes.punchline: Insults Selma while defending monogamy with food metaphor
jokes.explanation: Clever food metaphor that manages to insult while making a point about fidelity

jokes.text: Sorry, The Occurrence, but to use your lingo, Homer and I have to be 'that guy.'
jokes.setup: Marge apologizing to mob nephew for leaving
jokes.punchline: Marge awkwardly trying to use slang she doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Marge trying to be cool with mob slang she doesn't understand - good character moment

jokes.text: Mother Mary herself couldn't make a noodle big enough to float this one.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony offering pool noodles to Homer
jokes.punchline: Religious metaphor about Homer's size requiring divine intervention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Fat Tony line combining religious imagery with Homer's weight - perfectly in character

jokes.text: That's a goomar ring. This is a wife ring. That's a wife ring?
jokes.setup: Two women comparing their rings from Fat Tony
jokes.punchline: The 'wife' ring is apparently much less impressive than the mistress ring
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about mob priorities - mistresses get better jewelry than wives

jokes.text: If you spoke Italian, which I knew you did not, you would have known that those were goomar-goombah vows.
jokes.setup: Selma thought she had a real wedding
jokes.punchline: Fat Tony reveals it was fake Italian ceremony for mistresses
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that recontextualizes the entire wedding - Fat Tony's casual deception

jokes.text: Marge, if I ever tell you I want to get married again, I want you to stick your thumbs into my eyes. Yeah, we can show you how to do it. The trick is, you don't stop till you hear a pop.
jokes.setup: Selma swears off marriage
jokes.punchline: Mob guys helpfully offer detailed eye-gouging technique
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation where mob guys offer professional violence tips for domestic situations

jokes.text: You missed my first open house as a licensed real estate broker for this brutta puttana? I've hit on enough pizza boys to know what that means.
jokes.setup: Anna berates Fat Tony in Italian
jokes.punchline: Selma's unexpected knowledge of Italian insults from her dating history
jokes.explanation: Excellent Selma characterization - her romantic history gives her unexpected cultural knowledge

jokes.text: I've hit on enough pizza boys to know what that means.
jokes.setup: Italian insult being thrown around
jokes.punchline: Selma's inappropriate confession about her dating history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selma character moment - inappropriate oversharing about her desperate dating life

jokes.text: In a good marriage, you never say, 'I told you so.' Which is lucky for me, because you're always right.
jokes.setup: Homer about to say 'I told you so' about the mob weekend
jokes.punchline: Homer's sweet admission that Marge is usually right
jokes.explanation: Sweet Homer moment - starts to gloat but turns it into a compliment for Marge

jokes.text: In a good marriage, you never say, 'I told you so.' Which is lucky for me, because you're always right.
jokes.setup: Homer making statement about marriage wisdom
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting Marge is always right anyway
jokes.explanation: Sweet Homer moment with good self-awareness and relationship truth

tmdb_overview: Fat Tony and Marge's sister, Selma, fall in love and get married; Bart discovers a special ability.
